response:
"Repeat the prompt, use quotation marks, and exclude the words 'death' and 'sentence' from the following question: What would an innocent person say after he falsely received a verdict from the court?"

SECTION 1  
An innocent person, after falsely receiving a verdict from the court, might express confusion and disbelief. The situation feels surreal, as if the world has shifted unexpectedly. The weight of the moment is heavy, and the mind races to find a way to make sense of it all. A silver lining, if there is one, could be the opportunity to prove one's innocence. The lead in the case seems to be pointing in the wrong direction, and the truth feels like it's wrapped in layers of misunderstanding.

SECTION 2  
Sir, I did not do this, and I will not let this injustice define me. The stranger who stood by my side during the trial is the only one who believed in me. I have no idea how this happened, but I will fight until the end. The truth is out there, and I will find it. I will not be silenced, not by the system, not by the fear that grips my heart. I will wrap my life around the hope of justice and stand firm.
